behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies directly: correctness depends on real neural translation output quality (fluency + semantic preservation), which a stub cannot validate. MULTI-STEP does not apply because this is one model decision, not a chained agent pipeline. Rejected Workflow because behavior text only asks about single translation output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation API on a fixed multilingual evaluation set; assert quality thresholds (e.g., semantic similarity/human rubric) for meaning preservation and grammatical coherence, and fail on degradations across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: On translation API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and still returns success.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real HTTP 503 behavior and client error mapping can differ from mocks; a pure stub can miss failures in status handling, retry/fallback trigger, and transport-layer behavior. Rejected Agentic because this behavior validates error/fallback orchestration, not model output quality. Rejected Workflow because only one decision path (primary fail -> fallback) is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real HTTP test endpoint that returns 503 and run the service end-to-end; verify caller receives a fallback translation (non-error response) and the service does not propagate 503.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is module handoff between fallback translator and SQLite repository/schema, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA does not require OS/network/subprocess beyond local in-process DB wiring. Rejected System because behavior text is limited to local SQLite lookup correctness.
behaviors.test_description: With a real local SQLite test DB seeded with known phrase->translation rows, call fallback translator through service module boundaries and assert exact returned translation for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes character-level edit distance between primary/fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans computation plus persistence, so cross-module wiring is part of the failure boundary (metric calculator -> storage). LLM-DEP is not required because deterministic strings can exercise correctness. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about edit-distance math/storage, not judging model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic primary/fallback strings through the quality-tracking path, assert exact edit-distance value, and verify the metric record is persisted in the configured store.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff across modules (primary result selection + background quality logging). LLM output quality is not the assertion target, so Agentic is not required. Rejected Workflow because no multiple real model decisions are sequenced; this is service orchestration around one request.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with deterministic primary and fallback providers plus real async job/executor used in app; assert response uses primary output immediately and a quality-comparison log/metric appears after background processing.
